name: Proserpina
shortDescription: Proserpina is the Roman goddess of springtime, and later, queen of the underworld. She is the daughter of Ceres and the wife of Pluto.
description: Proserpina is the Roman goddess of springtime, agriculture, and the queen of the underworld. She is the daughter of Ceres, the goddess of agriculture, and Jupiter. Her most famous myth involves her abduction by Pluto, the god of the underworld, who desired her as his wife. While gathering flowers, Proserpina was seized by Pluto and taken to the underworld. Ceres, in her grief, caused the earth to become barren, leading to famine. Jupiter intervened, decreeing that Proserpina would spend part of the year with Pluto in the underworld (autumn and winter) and the rest with her mother on earth (spring and summer). This myth explains the changing seasons. As queen of the underworld, Proserpina's role evolved to encompass death and regeneration, reflecting the cyclical nature of life and the seasons. She was associated with fertility and the growth of crops, as well as the mysteries of the afterlife. Her worship was often intertwined with that of Ceres, and their joint cult was important in Roman religious life. Temples dedicated to Proserpina were found throughout the Roman world, and festivals were held in her honor, particularly during the spring and autumn equinoxes. Proserpina embodies the duality of life and death, representing both the joy of spring and the somberness of winter.

sources.title: The Roman Cultural Revolution: From Republic to Empire
sources.author: Thomas Habinek
